Also, I notice there's a gray block with a black center at the left side of the maze (rows 20-24, cols 14-18). This is a new object that appeared! Let me check the segmentation.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: blue block at rows 8-12, cols 26-30
# Continue RIGHT to reach end

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24:
        print(f"Blue block after RIGHTx4: boundary={n['boundary']}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['boundary'][0][0] == 63 and n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"HUD blue: pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'w' and n['boundary'][0][0] == 63:
        print(f"HUD gray: pixels={n['pixels']}")
print(f"board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

# Now try SPACE at end position

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24:
        print(f"Blue block after SPACE: boundary={n['boundary']}")

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['boundary'][0][0] == 63 and n['pixels'] > 10:
        print(f"HUD blue: pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'w' and n['boundary'][0][0] == 63:
        print(f"HUD gray: pixels={n['pixels']}")
print(f"board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"done={last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
